```{r}
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_17_11_2025/CML_males_castrés/Dotplot_enrich_MALEcas_CML_KIvsWT.png", plot = D, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)

# Enrichment avec l'utilisation de la base de donnée KEGG
ekegg <- enrichKEGG(gene         = genes_or_entrez,
                    organism     = "mmu",
                    pvalueCutoff = 0.05)

ekegg
head(ekegg@result)
dotplot(ekegg, showCategory = 20) # showCategory nous permet d'indique le nbre de pathway que nous voulons mettre sur notre dotplot
D1 <- dotplot(ekegg, showCategory = 20) + ggtitle(" KEGG enrichment (KI vs WT, males castrés)")
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_17_11_2025/CML_males_castrés/Dotplot_enrich_KEGG_MALEcas_CML_KIvsWT.png", plot = D1, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)

# Enrichment avec l'utilisation de la base de donnée KEGG
#BiocManager::install("ReactomePA")
library(ReactomePA)
ereact <- enrichPathway(gene = genes_or_entrez, organism="mouse", pvalueCutoff = 0.05, qvalueCutoff = 0.05)
ereact
dotplot(ereact, showCategory = 2)
barplot(ereact, showCategory=2) # 5 ici parce qu'il y a eu que 5 enrichissements
D2 <- dotplot(ereact, showCategory = 2) + ggtitle(" REACTOME enrichment (KI vs WT, males castrés)")
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_17_11_2025/CML_males_castrés/Dotplot_enrich_REACTOME_MALEcas_CML_KIvsWT.png", plot = D2, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)
B <- barplot(ereact, showCategory=2) + ggtitle(" REACTOME enrichment (KI vs WT, males castrés)")
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_17_11_2025/CML_males_castrés/Barplot_enrich_REACTOME_MALEcas_CML_KIvsWT.png", plot = B, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)

# Exemple GSEA en utilisant msigdbr C2
m_df <- msigdbr(species = "Mus musculus", category = "C2")
m_df
pathways <- split(m_df$gene_symbol, m_df$gs_name)
pathways
ranks <- markers_malecas_KI_vs_WT$avg_log2FC
ranks
names(ranks) <- rownames(markers_malecas_KI_vs_WT)
ranks <- sort(ranks, decreasing = TRUE)
# run fgsea
fg <- fgsea(pathways = pathways, stats = ranks, nperm = 10000)
head(fg[order(padj), ])
# Visualisation
top_fg <- fg[order(fg$padj), ][1:20, ]  # top 20: les 20 pathways les plus enrichies
top_fg
B1 <- ggplot(top_fg, aes(x = reorder(pathway, NES), y = NES, fill = padj)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "red", high = "blue", name = "padj") +
  labs(title = "Top 20 pathways enrichis (GSEA - fgsea)",
       x = "Pathway", y = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)") +
  theme_classic()
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_17_11_2025/CML_males_castrés/Barplot_enrich_GSEA_MALEcas_CML_KIvsWT.png", plot = B1, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)

# plotEnrichment(pathways[["JECHLINGER_EPITHELIAL_TO_MESENCHYMAL_TRANSITION_DN"]], ranks) # Cela trace la courbe de GSEA : comment les gènes d’un pathway sont distribués parmi les plus up ou down-régulés.
```

# Pour les EC
```{r}
data_male
table(data_male$orig.ident)
table(Idents(data_male))
# Pour les adipocytes bruns
# Extraction des types cellulaires présentent dans data.integrated2
table(Idents(data.integrated2))
data.integrated2
data.integrated2$celltype <- Idents(data.integrated2)
table(data.integrated2$celltype)
celltype_of_interest <- "EC"
data_EC <- subset(data_male, subset = celltype == celltype_of_interest)
data_EC
# Extraction de DEG exprimés entre KI et WT (chez les mâles)
table(data.integrated2$genotype)
Idents(data_EC) <- "genotype"  
levels(Idents(data_EC))
markers_male_KI_vs_WT <- FindMarkers(
  data_EC,
  ident.1 = "KI",
  ident.2 = "WT",
  logfc.threshold = 0.25,
  min.pct = 0.1
)
head(markers_male_KI_vs_WT)
write.xlsx(markers_male_KI_vs_WT,
             "/home/afall/r-docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyses_SnRNA_01_2026/Demande_06_02_2026/DEG_EC_KI_vs_WT_MALE_dataintegrated2.xlsx",
             rowNames = TRUE)

library(ggrepel)

markers_male_KI_vs_WT$gene <- rownames(markers_male_KI_vs_WT)

# Création colonne "signification" selon les seuils
markers_male_KI_vs_WT$signification <- "NS"
markers_male_KI_vs_WT$signification[markers_male_KI_vs_WT$p_val_adj < 0.05 &
                                   markers_male_KI_vs_WT$avg_log2FC > 0.25] <- "Up in KI"
markers_male_KI_vs_WT$signification[markers_male_KI_vs_WT$p_val_adj < 0.05 &
                                   markers_male_KI_vs_WT$avg_log2FC < -0.25] <- "Up in WT"
# Sélectionner les gènes à annoter (les 20 plus différentiels)
genes_to_label <- subset(markers_male_KI_vs_WT, p_val_adj < 0.05 & abs(avg_log2FC) > 0.5)
genes_to_label <- genes_to_label[order(abs(genes_to_label$avg_log2FC), decreasing = TRUE), ][1:20, ]
genes_to_label
# Tous les points = markers_femelle_KI_vs_WT
 volcano <- ggplot(markers_male_KI_vs_WT, aes(x = avg_log2FC, y = -log10(p_val_adj), color = signification)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.5) +
  
  # Annotations = seulement diff_genes
 geom_text_repel(data = genes_to_label, aes(label = gene), size = 3, max.overlaps = 20) +
  
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Up in KI" = "red", "Up in WT" = "blue", "NS" = "grey70")) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.25, 0.25), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Volcano plot: KI vs WT (Male EC)",
       x = "log2 Fold Change",
       y = "-log10(p_adj)") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14)
volcano
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Mâles/EC/Volcano_male_EC_KIvsWT.png", plot = volcano, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)

```


###### Analyse des grands types cellulaires (pathways)
```{r}
### PATHWAY
# Récupérer la liste de gènes différentiellements exprimés 
sig <- subset(markers_male_KI_vs_WT, p_val_adj < 0.05 & abs(avg_log2FC) > 0.5)
genes_sym <- rownames(sig) # récupération du symbol des gènes 
# Conversion des identifiants d'entrée ( ici nous transformons les gènes en indentifiants d'entrée c'est à dire des chiffres)
conv <- bitr(rownames(markers_male_KI_vs_WT), fromType="SYMBOL", toType="ENTREZID", OrgDb="org.Mm.eg.db")
genes_or_entrez <- conv$ENTREZID[conv$SYMBOL %in% genes_sym]
universe_entrez <- conv$ENTREZID

# Enrichment avec l'utilisation de la base de donnée GO
#ego <- enrichGO(gene = genes_or_entrez,
                #universe = universe_entrez,
                #OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
                #keyType = "ENTREZID",
                #ont = "BP",
                #pAdjustMethod = "BH",
                #pvalueCutoff = 0.2,
                #qvalueCutoff = 0.5)
#ego   # avec le code de eco nous n'avons rien trouver parceque les filtres effectués étaient trop strictes

ego_all <- enrichGO(
  gene = genes_or_entrez,
  OrgDb = org.Mm.eg.db,
  keyType = "ENTREZID",
  ont = "BP",
  pvalueCutoff = 1,
  qvalueCutoff = 1
)
ego_all
head(ego_all@result)

# Visualisation 
D <- dotplot(ego_all, showCategory = 20) + ggtitle("GO BP enrichment (KI vs WT, males)")
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Mâles/EC/Dotplot_enrich_MALE_EC_KIvsWT.png", plot = D, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)

# Enrichment avec l'utilisation de la base de donnée KEGG
ekegg <- enrichKEGG(gene         = genes_or_entrez,
                    organism     = "mmu",
                    pvalueCutoff = 0.05)

ekegg
head(ekegg@result)
dotplot(ekegg, showCategory = 20) # showCategory nous permet d'indique le nbre de pathway que nous voulons mettre sur notre dotplot
D1 <- dotplot(ekegg, showCategory = 20) + ggtitle(" KEGG enrichment (KI vs WT, males)")
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Mâles/EC/Dotplot_enrich_KEGG_MALE_EC_KIvsWT.png", plot = D1, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)

# Enrichment avec l'utilisation de la base de donnée KEGG
#BiocManager::install("ReactomePA")
library(ReactomePA)
ereact <- enrichPathway(gene = genes_or_entrez, organism="mouse", pvalueCutoff = 0.05, qvalueCutoff = 0.1)
ereact
dotplot(ereact, showCategory = 20)
barplot(ereact, showCategory=20) # 5 ici parce qu'il y a eu que 5 enrichissements
D2 <- dotplot(ereact, showCategory = 20) + ggtitle(" REACTOME enrichment (KI vs WT, males)")
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Mâles/EC/Dotplot_enrich_REACTOME_MALE_EC_KIvsWT.png", plot = D2, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)
B <- barplot(ereact, showCategory=20) + ggtitle(" REACTOME enrichment (KI vs WT, males)")
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Mâles/EC/Barplot_enrich_REACTOME_MALE_EC_KIvsWT.png", plot = B, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)

# Exemple GSEA en utilisant msigdbr C2
m_df <- msigdbr(species = "Mus musculus", category = "C2")
m_df
pathways <- split(m_df$gene_symbol, m_df$gs_name)
pathways
ranks <- markers_male_KI_vs_WT$avg_log2FC
ranks
names(ranks) <- rownames(markers_male_KI_vs_WT)
ranks <- sort(ranks, decreasing = TRUE)
# run fgsea
fg <- fgsea(pathways = pathways, stats = ranks, nperm = 10000)
head(fg[order(padj), ])
# Visualisation
top_fg <- fg[order(fg$padj), ][1:20, ]  # top 20: les 20 pathways les plus enrichies
top_fg
B1 <- ggplot(top_fg, aes(x = reorder(pathway, NES), y = NES, fill = padj)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "red", high = "blue", name = "padj") +
  labs(title = "Top 20 pathways enrichis (GSEA - fgsea)",
       x = "Pathway", y = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)") +
  theme_classic()
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Mâles/EC/Barplot_enrich_GSEA_MALE_EC_KIvsWT.png", plot = B1, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)

```


##### Réclustering du cluster des EC
```{r}
data_EC <- FindNeighbors(data_EC, dims = 1:30)

# résolution à 0.5
data_EC <- FindClusters(data_CML, resolution = 0.5)
head(Idents(data_CML), 5)
# Les Umaps
plotCML<- DimPlot(data_CML, reduction="umap", label=TRUE, pt.size=0.5)
plotCML
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_du_03_11_2025/Umap_CML_res_05.jpg", plot = plotCML, height = 7, width = 7, dpi = 100)

# extraction des marqueurs
find_all_markers <- function(data_CML) {
  clusters <- levels(Idents(data_CML))
  all_markers <- data.frame()
  for (cluster in clusters) {
    cat("Traitement du cluster", cluster, "\n")
    markers <- FindMarkers(data_CML, ident.1 = cluster, min.pct = 0.25)
    markers_df <- as.data.frame(markers)
    markers_df <- rownames_to_column(markers_df, var = "gene")
    top_genes <- markers_df %>%
      arrange(desc(avg_log2FC)) %>%
      head(50)
    cluster_genes <- top_genes$gene
    if (ncol(all_markers) == 0) {
      all_markers <- data.frame(cluster_genes)
      colnames(all_markers) <- paste("Cluster", cluster)
    } else {
      all_markers <- cbind(all_markers, cluster_genes)
      colnames(all_markers)[ncol(all_markers)] <- paste("Cluster", cluster)
    }
    
  }
  write.xlsx(all_markers,
             "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_du_03_11_2025/Genes_marqueurs_CML_res05.xlsx",
             row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(all_markers)
}

# Appliquer la fonction
all_markers_list <- find_all_markers(data_CML)
all_markers_list

# Umap par condition présent clez les CML
data_CML_male <- subset(data_CML, subset = orig.ident %in% c("B6_M_WT", "B6_M_KI"))
data_CML_male 
D<- DimPlot(data_CML_male, reduction = "umap", group.by = "orig.ident")
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_du_03_11_2025/Umap_CML_res_005.jpg", plot = D, height = 7, width = 7, dpi = 100)

# Sélectionner les cellules WT mâles
Idents(data_CML_male) <- data_CML_male$orig.ident  # définir l'identité
# plot des Echantillons males wt et mutés
plotWT<- DimPlot(data_CML, reduction = "umap", 
        cells.highlight = list(
          WhichCells(data_CML, expression = orig.ident == "B6_M_KI")
        ), 
        cols.highlight = c("green"), 
        cols = "gray")
plotWT
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_du_03_11_2025/UMAP_CML_échantillons_Males_KI_SBalbc.jpg", plot = plotWT, height = 7, width = 7, dpi = 100)

plotKI<- DimPlot(data_CML, reduction = "umap", 
        cells.highlight = list(
          WhichCells(data_CML, expression = orig.ident == "B6_M_WT")
        ), 
        cols.highlight = c("yellow"), 
        cols = "gray")
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_du_03_11_2025/UMAP_CML_échantillons_Males_WT_SBalbc.jpg", plot = plotKI, height = 7, width = 7, dpi = 100)

colnames(data_CML@meta.data)
T5 <- table(data_CML@meta.data$seurat_clusters,
            data_CML@meta.data$orig.ident)
T5
df5 <- as.data.frame.matrix(T5)  
df5 <- tibble::rownames_to_column(df5, var = "Cluster")
df5
library(openxlsx)

write.xlsx(df5,
  file = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_du_03_11_2025/Noyaux_par_cluster_et_par_condition_CML_res05.xlsx",
  rowNames = FALSE
)
T6 <- table(data_CML_male@meta.data$seurat_clusters,
            data_CML_male@meta.data$orig.ident)
T6
df6 <- as.data.frame.matrix(T6)  
df6 <- tibble::rownames_to_column(df6, var = "Cluster")
df6

write.xlsx(df6,
  file = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_du_03_11_2025/Noyaux_par_cluster_KIvsWT_CML_res05.xlsx",
  rowNames = FALSE)

```

```{r}
# résolution à 0.05

data_CML <- FindNeighbors(data_CML, dims = 1:30)
data_CML <- FindClusters(data_CML, resolution = 0.05)
head(Idents(data_CML), 5)
# Les Umaps
plotCML1<- DimPlot(data_CML, reduction="umap", label=TRUE, pt.size=0.5)
plotCML1
ggsave(filename = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_du_03_11_2025/Umap_CML_res_005.jpg", plot = plotCML1, height = 7, width = 7, dpi = 100)

colnames(data_CML@meta.data)
T7 <- table(data_CML@meta.data$seurat_clusters,
            data_CML@meta.data$orig.ident)
T7
df7 <- as.data.frame.matrix(T7)  
df7 <- tibble::rownames_to_column(df7, var = "Cluster")
df7

write.xlsx(df7,
  file = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_du_03_11_2025/Noyaux_par_cluster_et_par_condition_CML_res005.xlsx",
  rowNames = FALSE
)

#cells_CML <- colnames(data_CML)
data_CML@reductions$umap <- data.integrated2@reductions$umap
data_CML@reductions$umap
# tableau global avec clusters CML
umap_global <- data.frame(Embeddings(data.integrated2, "umap"))
umap_global
umap_global$highlight <- ifelse(rownames(umap_global) %in% colnames(data_CML), "CML", "Other")

# Ajouter cluster info pour les CML uniquement
umap_global$cluster_CML <- NA
umap_global[colnames(data_CML), "cluster_CML"] <- as.character(Idents(data_CML))

library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)

ggplot(umap_global, aes(umap_1, umap_2)) +
  geom_point(data = subset(umap_global, highlight == "Other"),
             color = "grey90", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(data = subset(umap_global, highlight == "CML"),
             aes(color = cluster_CML), size = 1.2, alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "plasma", na.value = "grey90") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right") +
  ggtitle("Clusters CML sur fond UMAP global")
ggsave("/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_du_30_10_2025/UMAP_CML_res005_sur_fond_global.png", width = 7, height = 6, dpi = 300)
### Nbre de noyau par cluster des CML et par condition expérimentale

colnames(data_CML@meta.data)
T4 <- table(data_CML@meta.data$seurat_clusters,
            data_CML@meta.data$orig.ident)
T4
df4 <- as.data.frame.matrix(T4)  
df4 <- tibble::rownames_to_column(df4, var = "Cluster")
df4
library(openxlsx)

write.xlsx(df4,
  file = "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_du_30_10_2025/Noyaux_par_cluster_et_par_condition_CML_res005.xlsx",
  rowNames = FALSE
)
Embeddings(data.integrated2, "umap")
umap_df <- data.frame(Embeddings(data.integrated2, "umap"))
head(umap_df)
umap_df$celltype <- data.integrated2$celltype
umap_df$seurat_clusters <- data.integrated2$seurat_clusters
cells_CML <- colnames(data_CML)
umap_df$CML_flag <- ifelse(rownames(umap_df) %in% cells_CML, "CML", "Other")
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(umap_df, aes(umap_1, umap_2)) +
  geom_point(data = subset(umap_df, CML_flag == "Other"),
             color = "grey90", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(data = subset(umap_df, CML_flag == "CML"),
             aes(color = seurat_clusters), size = 1.2, alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "plasma") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Sous-ensemble CML sur fond UMAP global")


# extraction des marqueurs
find_all_markers <- function(data_CML) {
  clusters <- levels(Idents(data_CML))
  all_markers <- data.frame()
  for (cluster in clusters) {
    cat("Traitement du cluster", cluster, "\n")
    markers <- FindMarkers(data_CML, ident.1 = cluster, min.pct = 0.25)
    markers_df <- as.data.frame(markers)
    markers_df <- rownames_to_column(markers_df, var = "gene")
    top_genes <- markers_df %>%
      arrange(desc(avg_log2FC)) %>%
      head(50)
    cluster_genes <- top_genes$gene
    if (ncol(all_markers) == 0) {
      all_markers <- data.frame(cluster_genes)
      colnames(all_markers) <- paste("Cluster", cluster)
    } else {
      all_markers <- cbind(all_markers, cluster_genes)
      colnames(all_markers)[ncol(all_markers)] <- paste("Cluster", cluster)
    }
    
  }
  write.xlsx(all_markers,
             "/home/afall/r_docker/Analyses_SnRNA_10_2025/Sans_Balbc/Version_nautilus/Analyse_différentielle_types_cellulaires/Demandes_du_03_11_2025/Genes_marqueurs_CML_res005.xlsx",
             row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(all_markers)
}

# Appliquer la fonction
all_markers_list <- find_all_markers(data_CML)
all_markers_list

```
